enrichment.short_description: A free, Apache-licensed Java web framework and suite of business applications for ERP, CRM, and e-commerce.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Apache OFBiz is a free, open-source Java ERP suite with accounting, CRM, order management, warehousing, and manufacturing modules.

features.title: Accounting
features.description: Includes general ledger, accounts receivable, accounts payable, and fixed assets management.

features.title: CRM, Order Management & E-Commerce
features.description: Handles customer relationship management alongside order processing and online storefront capabilities.

features.title: Warehousing and Inventory
features.description: Supports supply chain fulfillment and inventory tracking across the warehouse.

features.title: Manufacturing & MRP
features.description: Provides manufacturing resource planning tools for production operations.

features.title: Product and catalog management
features.description: Manage product catalogs, promotions, and pricing rules.

features.title: Entity, service, and widget engine
features.description: Built as a Java-based web framework including an entity engine, a service engine, and a widget-based UI, giving developers a common architecture to extend or enhance the platform.

pros: Completely free with no licensing fees, under the Apache License 2.0
Covers a broad suite of business applications (accounting, CRM, order management, warehousing, manufacturing) in one framework
Common underlying architecture makes it possible for developers to extend or enhance the platform
Backed by The Apache Software Foundation as a long-running top-level project
cons: Requires the Java Development Kit and manual setup via README instructions, implying developer effort to install and maintain
No hosted/managed version mentioned, so users must self-host and self-support
Official site does not document supported operating systems or deployment environments beyond Java

faqs.question: Is Apache OFBiz free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. OFBiz has no upfront costs and no licensing fees, and it is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0.

faqs.question: Who maintains Apache OFBiz?
faqs.answer: It is maintained by The Apache Software Foundation and has been an Apache top-level project for 10 years.

faqs.question: What business functions does OFBiz cover?
faqs.answer: Its core modules include Accounting, CRM/Order Management/E-Commerce, Warehousing and Inventory, and Manufacturing & MRP.

faqs.question: What do I need to install Apache OFBiz?
faqs.answer: Installation requires the Java Development Kit, following the setup instructions provided in the project's README file.

use_cases.title: Multi-module ERP for growing businesses
use_cases.description: Companies can run accounting, CRM, order management, warehousing, and manufacturing on one common framework without licensing fees.
use_cases.audience: Businesses across industries needing an extensible, self-hosted ERP

use_cases.title: Custom ERP development platform
use_cases.description: Developers use OFBiz's entity engine, service engine, and widget-based UI as a base architecture to build or extend tailored business applications.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Apache OFBiz?
paragraphs: Apache OFBiz is described on its official site as a suite of business applications flexible enough to be used across any industry, built on a common architecture that lets developers easily extend or enhance it.

Technically, it is a Java-based web framework that includes an entity engine, a service engine, and a widget-based UI, and it is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 with no upfront costs or licensing fees.

It is maintained by The Apache Software Foundation and has been an Apache top-level project for 10 years, with core modules covering Accounting, CRM/Order Management/E-Commerce, Warehousing and Inventory, and Manufacturing & MRP.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Apache OFBiz is for
paragraphs: OFBiz addresses both developers and business users, with dedicated onboarding resources for each group, according to the official site.

It suits organizations that want a free, extensible, self-hosted ERP framework covering accounting, order management, warehousing, and manufacturing, as well as developers who want a common architecture to build custom business applications on top of.

enrichment.short_description: A free, open-source Tier II ERP/CRM/SCM system built on Java, ZK, and OSGi, maintained by a volunteer community.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: iDempiere is a free, open-source Tier II ERP/CRM/SCM system with enterprise accounting, multi-entity support, and Java/ZK/OSGi architecture.

features.title: Enterprise-grade accounting
features.description: Provides accounting capabilities suited to enterprise use.

features.title: Multi-entity support
features.description: Supports multiple business entities from a single login.

features.title: Scalable architecture
features.description: Scales from small servers up to environments serving hundreds of concurrent users.

features.title: Open technology stack
features.description: Built on Java, ZK, and OSGi, with compatibility for both Oracle and PostgreSQL databases, using Maven for project management.

features.title: LTS releases
features.description: The iDempiere 13 'Orion' release is a long-term support (LTS) version with security enhancements and performance improvements.

pros: Completely free and open-source, with the project stating it 'will be open forever'
Avoids vendor lock-in associated with proprietary ERP software
Maintained by an active volunteer community of specialists, implementors, developers, and end-users worldwide
Scales from small servers to hundreds of concurrent users
Supports both Oracle and PostgreSQL databases
cons: Relies on volunteer community maintenance rather than a dedicated commercial vendor
Official site does not document specific supported languages or company/headquarters details
Requires familiarity with Java, ZK, OSGi, and Maven-based setup

faqs.question: Is iDempiere free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, iDempiere is completely free and open-source, and the project states it will remain open forever.

faqs.question: When was iDempiere founded?
faqs.answer: iDempiere has been operating since its founding in 2011.

faqs.question: Who maintains iDempiere?
faqs.answer: A volunteer community of subject matter specialists, implementors, developers, and end-users from various countries maintains the project.

faqs.question: What technology does iDempiere run on?
faqs.answer: It is built on Java, ZK, and OSGi, and is compatible with Oracle and PostgreSQL databases, using Maven for project management.

faqs.question: What is the latest iDempiere release?
faqs.answer: The latest mentioned release is iDempiere 13 'Orion,' a long-term support (LTS) version with security enhancements and performance improvements.

use_cases.title: Multi-entity enterprise ERP
use_cases.description: Organizations managing multiple business entities can operate them from a single login with enterprise-grade accounting.
use_cases.audience: Mid-size to large organizations with multiple business units

use_cases.title: Vendor lock-in-free ERP adoption
use_cases.description: Companies seeking affordable ERP without proprietary software constraints can deploy iDempiere as a free, permanently open alternative.
use_cases.audience: Cost-conscious businesses worldwide avoiding proprietary licensing

long_form_blocks.heading: What is iDempiere?
paragraphs: iDempiere is described on its official site as a powerful, Tier II, open-source ERP/CRM/SCM system that has been operating since its founding in 2011.
The software is completely free with no licensing costs, and the project emphasizes that iDempiere will be open forever, protecting users from vendor lock-in.

It is built on Java, ZK, and OSGi, compatible with Oracle and PostgreSQL databases, and maintained by a skilled volunteer community of specialists, implementors, developers, and end-users from various countries and professional backgrounds.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who iDempiere is for
paragraphs: iDempiere serves companies worldwide seeking affordable enterprise resource planning solutions without proprietary software constraints.
It fits organizations needing enterprise-grade accounting and multi-entity management, and that can scale from small servers to deployments with hundreds of concurrent users.

enrichment.short_description: A free, open-source ERP/CRM/MFG/SCM/POS business suite licensed under GPL-2.0 and developed by a global community.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ADempiere is a free, open-source GPL-licensed ERP suite covering CRM, manufacturing, supply chain, and point of sale.

features.title: ERP, CRM, MFG, SCM & POS modules
features.description: A business suite spanning enterprise resource planning, customer relationship management, manufacturing, supply chain management, and point of sale.

features.title: Business process management
features.description: Tools for managing and automating core business processes.

features.title: Material management
features.description: Functionality for managing inventory and materials across the supply chain.

features.title: Modern Vue-based UI
features.description: A newly developed user interface built with JavaScript and Vue.

features.title: Container-native deployment
features.description: Supports Docker, Helm charts, and a Kubernetes Operator for deployment.

features.title: HR, warehouse & financial modules
features.description: Includes specialized modules for HR/payroll, warehouse management, distribution, and financial operations.

pros: Completely free and open-source under the GPL-2.0 license
Broad functional coverage spanning ERP, CRM, manufacturing, supply chain, and POS in one suite
Active community with a Discord server, GitHub repository (875+ stars, 795+ forks), and a nonprofit ADempiere Foundation overseeing governance
Modern container-native deployment options via Docker, Helm, and a Kubernetes Operator
Long-running project with thousands of commits and a track record of community conferences
cons: Requires self-hosting and technical expertise (Java, Docker/Kubernetes) to deploy and maintain
No official managed/hosted edition is described on the site
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed by the project
Latest documented release (3.9.4, December 2023) suggests a slower release cadence than commercial SaaS competitors

faqs.question: Is ADempiere free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, ADempiere is completely free and open-source, licensed under GPL-2.0.

faqs.question: What modules does ADempiere include?
faqs.answer: It covers ERP, CRM, manufacturing (MFG), supply chain management (SCM), and point of sale (POS), plus specialized HR, warehouse, and financial modules.

faqs.question: How is ADempiere deployed?
faqs.answer: It supports Docker, Helm charts, and a Kubernetes Operator, alongside a newer Vue-based user interface.

faqs.question: Who governs the ADempiere project?
faqs.answer: The ADempiere Foundation, an independent nonprofit, oversees development alongside a community council and board, with contributors coordinating via Discord and GitHub.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted multi-module ERP
use_cases.description: Run ERP, CRM, manufacturing, supply chain, and POS from one open-source suite without licensing fees.
use_cases.audience: Organizations wanting a free, extensible ERP they fully control

use_cases.title: Container-based ERP deployment
use_cases.description: Deploy and scale ADempiere using Docker, Helm, and Kubernetes Operator tooling.
use_cases.audience: DevOps and IT teams managing containerized infrastructure

use_cases.title: Community-driven ERP customization
use_cases.description: Extend or customize the platform through its GPL-licensed codebase and active contributor community.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ADempiere?
paragraphs: ADempiere is a free, open-source ERP/CRM/MFG/SCM/POS business suite developed in an open, community-driven "Bazaar" model and licensed under GPL-2.0.
The project includes a newly developed user interface built with JavaScript and Vue, and supports modern container-native deployment through Docker, Helm charts, and a Kubernetes Operator.
Governance is handled by the independent nonprofit ADempiere Foundation, supported by a community council and board, with an active contributor base coordinating through Discord and GitHub.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ADempiere is for
paragraphs: ADempiere suits organizations that want a free, self-hosted ERP suite covering business processes, materials management, manufacturing, supply chain, and point of sale without ongoing license costs.
It also fits developers and implementors comfortable working with a Java-based, GPL-licensed codebase who want to customize or extend the platform as part of its open community.

enrichment.short_description: An all-in-one HR management system covering employee management, recruitment, onboarding, leave, and performance.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OrangeHRM offers a free open-source Starter edition plus a modular paid Advanced edition for HR, recruitment, and performance management.

features.title: Employee Management
features.description: Centralized HR administration and employee records management.

features.title: Recruitment & Applicant Tracking
features.description: An ATS module to manage job postings and candidate pipelines.

features.title: Onboarding
features.description: Tools to manage new-hire onboarding workflows.

features.title: Leave Management
features.description: PTO and leave request tracking and approvals.

features.title: Time & Attendance Tracking
features.description: Tracks employee working hours and attendance.

features.title: Performance Management
features.description: Tools for reviews, goals, and career development tracking.

features.title: OrangeHRM AI
features.description: Listed intelligent automation capabilities within the platform.

features.title: Reporting & Analytics
features.description: Built-in reporting across HR modules.

pros: Free, open-source Starter edition with core HR functionality at no cost
Modular Advanced pricing lets organizations pay only for the modules they need
Available as both cloud-hosted and on-premise deployments
30-day free trial offered for the Advanced edition
Claims over 5 million users on its site
cons: Advanced edition pricing is not published and requires a custom quote
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site
Supported languages are not documented on the site

faqs.question: Is OrangeHRM free?
faqs.answer: Yes, OrangeHRM offers a completely free, open-source Starter edition alongside a paid Advanced edition.

faqs.question: Does OrangeHRM offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 30-day free trial is offered for the Advanced edition.

faqs.question: How is OrangeHRM Advanced priced?
faqs.answer: Advanced uses a modular, pick-and-choose module pricing model; exact prices require contacting sales for a quote.

faqs.question: Can OrangeHRM be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, OrangeHRM supports both cloud-hosted and on-premise deployment options.

use_cases.title: Core HR administration
use_cases.description: Manage employee records, leave, and attendance in one system.
use_cases.audience: HR teams at small to mid-sized organizations

use_cases.title: Recruitment and onboarding
use_cases.description: Run applicant tracking and structured onboarding workflows.

use_cases.title: Performance and career development
use_cases.description: Track goals, reviews, and training programs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OrangeHRM?
paragraphs: OrangeHRM is an all-in-one HR management system with modules spanning employee management, recruitment and onboarding, performance management, and leave management.
It is offered as a free, open-source Starter edition and as a paid, modular Advanced edition where organizations select and pay for only the modules they need, with a 30-day free trial available.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OrangeHRM is for
paragraphs: OrangeHRM suits organizations that want a free open-source starting point for core HR tasks, as well as larger teams that need to add modular, paid capabilities like advanced talent management and compensation as they grow.

name: Sentrifugo
skip_reason: Official domain (sentrifugo.com, http and https, www and non-www) refuses connections (dead host). Checked alternate sources (SourceForge project page, GitHub org github.com/sentrifugo): the last code update was October 2017 and the SourceForge listing data is from April 2017, indicating the project is abandoned/discontinued rather than just temporarily unreachable, so no current facts could be verified.

enrichment.short_description: A complete HR toolkit covering employee management, leave, attendance, payroll, performance reviews, and recruitment.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: IceHrm is an HR toolkit with a free open-source edition plus paid self-hosted Pro and fully managed Cloud editions.

features.title: Employee Management
features.description: Centralizes all employee data in one secure location.

features.title: Leave Management
features.description: Streamlines leave requests with automated workflows.

features.title: Time & Attendance
features.description: Tracks hours with punch-in/out, timesheets, and overtime calculations.

features.title: Payroll & Reports
features.description: Generates payroll reports with customizable salary components.

features.title: Performance Reviews
features.description: 360-degree evaluations with customizable review templates.

features.title: Recruitment
features.description: Job postings, candidate management, and interview scheduling.

features.title: Task Management
features.description: Assign and track tasks for employees.

pros: Free, self-hosted open-source edition available
Mobile apps available on the iOS App Store and Google Play Store
Choice of self-hosted (one-time cost) or fully managed cloud hosting
Cloud edition includes 99.9% uptime SLA, automatic updates, and daily backups
Used in 150+ countries per the vendor's site
cons: IceHrmPro self-hosted license is a $2,499 one-time cost, not published as a subscription
IceHrm Cloud pricing is custom/quote-only and not published
Founding year, company size, and supported languages are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Is IceHrm free?
faqs.answer: Yes, IceHrm offers a free, self-hosted open-source community edition.

faqs.question: What does IceHrmPro cost?
faqs.answer: IceHrmPro, the self-hosted paid edition, is listed at $2,499 as a one-time price.

faqs.question: Is there a cloud-hosted version of IceHrm?
faqs.answer: Yes, IceHrm Cloud is a fully managed hosting option with a 99.9% uptime SLA; pricing is custom.

faqs.question: Does IceHrm have mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, IceHrm has mobile apps on the iOS App Store and Google Play Store.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted HR management
use_cases.description: Deploy the open-source or Pro edition on your own infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Managed cloud HR
use_cases.description: Use IceHrm Cloud for fully managed hosting with backups and uptime SLA.
use_cases.audience: Teams that prefer not to manage hosting

use_cases.title: Attendance and leave tracking
use_cases.description: Automate punch-in/out, timesheets, and leave workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is IceHrm?
paragraphs: IceHrm is an HR toolkit covering employee management, leave, attendance, payroll and reports, performance reviews, recruitment, and task management.

It is offered in three editions: a free open-source self-hosted community edition, a paid self-hosted IceHrmPro edition ($2,499 one-time), and a fully managed IceHrm Cloud edition with custom pricing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who IceHrm is for
paragraphs: IceHrm fits organizations that want to self-host HR software for free or a one-time fee, as well as teams that prefer a fully managed cloud option with an uptime SLA and automatic backups.

enrichment.short_description: A 100% open-source HR and payroll platform built on the Frappe Framework, covering the full employee lifecycle.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Frappe HR is a 100% open-source HR and payroll solution, free to self-host or deployable on Frappe Cloud starting at $5/month.

features.title: Recruitment
features.description: Plan, publish, analyze, and evaluate hiring, through to offers.

features.title: Employee Lifecycle Management
features.description: Handles onboarding, exits, transfers, and promotions.

features.title: Shifts & Attendance
features.description: Mobile check-ins and roster management for shift-based attendance.

features.title: Leave Management
features.description: Supports complex leave policies and encashments.

features.title: Expense Management
features.description: Advance payouts and travel expense accounting.

features.title: Performance Management
features.description: Goals, KRAs, appraisals, and feedback.

features.title: Payroll
features.description: Diverse pay structure mapping with integrated accounting.

features.title: Payroll Tax & Reports
features.description: Regional tax configuration and reporting.

pros: 100% open source under the AGPL-3.0 license
8.2K GitHub stars, indicating an active community
Integrates with ERPNext accounting and supports REST APIs, webhooks, biometric and bank integrations
Can be self-hosted for free or deployed on Frappe Cloud starting at $5/month
Supports multi-company operations
cons: Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site
Frappe HR-specific cloud pricing is not separated from general Frappe Cloud hosting pricing
Supported languages are not documented on the site

faqs.question: Is Frappe HR open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, Frappe HR is 100% open source under the AGPL-3.0 license, with 8.2K GitHub stars.

faqs.question: Can Frappe HR be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can be self-hosted for free, or deployed on the managed Frappe Cloud platform.

faqs.question: How much does Frappe Cloud hosting cost?
faqs.answer: Frappe Cloud site plans start at $5/month, with server plans starting at $40/month; a 14-day free trial is available.

faqs.question: Does Frappe HR integrate with accounting?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with ERPNext accounting and supports REST APIs and webhooks for other integrations.

use_cases.title: Open-source HR self-hosting
use_cases.description: Deploy Frappe HR on your own infrastructure at no license cost.
use_cases.audience: Organizations wanting full control and no per-user fees

use_cases.title: Payroll and accounting integration
use_cases.description: Run payroll with integrated accounting via ERPNext.
use_cases.audience: Finance and HR teams already using Frappe/ERPNext

use_cases.title: Managed cloud hosting
use_cases.description: Host Frappe HR on Frappe Cloud instead of self-managing servers.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Frappe HR?
paragraphs: Frappe HR is a 100% open-source HR and payroll solution built on the Frappe Framework, covering recruitment, employee lifecycle management, shifts and attendance, leave, expenses, performance, and payroll.
It can be self-hosted for free or deployed on Frappe Cloud, which offers site hosting starting at $5/month and server hosting starting at $40/month with a 14-day free trial.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Frappe HR is for
paragraphs: Frappe HR suits organizations already using or considering ERPNext/Frappe Framework tools, and teams that want a fully open-source HR system without per-user SaaS pricing.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source applicant tracking system and recruiting CRM that you host yourself.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OpenCATS is a free, self-hosted, open-source applicant tracking system and recruiting CRM for staffing agencies and in-house recruiting teams.

features.title: Candidate management
features.description: Keeps candidate records, resumes, notes, status history, and recruiting activity in one open system.

features.title: Recruiting CRM
features.description: Supports staffing agencies, internal recruiting teams, and hiring workflows in a single platform.

features.title: Job requisition tracking
features.description: Tracks open job requisitions through the hiring process.

features.title: Submission management
features.description: Manages candidate submissions against open job requisitions.

features.title: Company and contact management
features.description: Stores company and contact records associated with recruiting activity.

features.title: Self-hosted deployment
features.description: Runs on infrastructure you control, so you can host the software yourself and inspect the code.

pros: Completely free and open-source
Self-hosted, so data stays under the organization's control
Source code can be inspected, since it is open source
Maintained in the open by users and contributors on GitHub
Active community support via GitHub and a Reddit community
cons: Requires self-hosting and technical setup rather than offering a managed SaaS option
No AI features are documented on the official site
No published integrations list on the official site
No mobile apps mentioned on the official site

faqs.question: Is OpenCATS free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, OpenCATS is free, community-maintained applicant tracking and recruiting CRM software.

faqs.question: Do I need to host OpenCATS myself?
faqs.answer: Yes, OpenCATS uses a self-hosted deployment model, meaning you install and run it on your own infrastructure.

faqs.question: Who maintains OpenCATS?
faqs.answer: OpenCATS is maintained in the open by users and contributors, with development happening on GitHub.

faqs.question: Where can I get support for OpenCATS?
faqs.answer: Support resources include installation documentation, GitHub Security Advisories, and a Reddit community (r/openCATS).

use_cases.title: Staffing agency candidate tracking
use_cases.description: Manage candidate records, submissions, and job requisitions across client placements.

use_cases.title: Internal recruiting management
use_cases.description: Track hiring activity, candidates, and job requisitions for an in-house recruiting team.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted ATS for data control
use_cases.description: Run a recruiting system on owned infrastructure without depending on a third-party vendor.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OpenCATS?
paragraphs: OpenCATS is a free, community-maintained applicant tracking system and recruiting CRM. It is described on its official site as offering "applicant tracking that stays in your hands," reflecting its self-hosted model where organizations host the software themselves and can inspect the code.

The platform centers on candidate management, keeping candidate records, resumes, notes, status history, and recruiting activity in one open system, alongside job requisition tracking, submission management, and company/contact management.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OpenCATS is for
paragraphs: OpenCATS is built for teams that need candidate management without vendor dependency, including staffing agencies and internal recruiting teams that want to self-host their applicant tracking system and maintain direct control over their data and code.

enrichment.short_description: AI-native recruiting CRM and ATS for executive search firms and recruiting agencies, starting at $149/user/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Recruiterflow is an AI-native ATS and CRM for recruiting agencies and executive search firms, with automation, sequences, and an open API.

features.title: ATS and CRM
features.description: Combines applicant tracking for candidate pipelines with CRM functionality for lead and client management.

features.title: Multichannel sequences
features.description: Runs outreach sequences across email, LinkedIn, and phone.

features.title: Recruitment and sales automation
features.description: Automates recruiting and sales workflows to reduce manual work.

features.title: Recruiterflow AI agents
features.description: A suite of built-in intelligent agents, including AIRA Notetaker which automatically summarizes key points from candidate calls.

features.title: AIRA Search
features.description: Natural language search technology for finding candidates or records.

features.title: Job Change Alerts
features.description: Tracks when candidates switch roles or companies.

features.title: Reporting and BI
features.description: Advanced reporting, Recruiterflow BI, and goals/leaderboards tracking.

features.title: Open API
features.description: Provides an open API for integrating with other tools.

pros: Rated 4.8 out of 5 on both G2 and Capterra
Used by 2,100+ search firms globally
SOC 2 and ISO 27001 certified, with GDPR compliance
Combines ATS and CRM functionality with sales automation in one platform
Includes AI-driven features such as AIRA Notetaker and natural-language AIRA Search
cons: Starting price of $149 per user/month is higher than many entry-level ATS tools
AI-focused AIRA plan uses custom, quote-only pricing rather than published rates
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: How much does Recruiterflow cost?
faqs.answer: The Platform plan is $149 per user per month, billed monthly or annually. The AIRA plan, which adds further AI agents, uses custom pricing.

faqs.question: Does Recruiterflow include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, Recruiterflow AI includes a suite of built-in intelligent agents, AIRA Search for natural language search, and AIRA Notetaker, which automatically summarizes candidate calls.

faqs.question: Is there an API available?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Platform plan includes API access.

faqs.question: What is Recruiterflow's review rating?
faqs.answer: Recruiterflow is rated 4.8 out of 5 on both G2 and Capterra.

faqs.question: Who uses Recruiterflow?
faqs.answer: Recruiterflow is used by executive search firms, recruiting agencies, and staffing companies; it reports 2,100+ search firms globally as users.

use_cases.title: Executive search pipeline management
use_cases.description: Track candidate pipelines and client relationships for retained search engagements.

use_cases.title: Agency outreach automation
use_cases.description: Run multichannel sequences across email, LinkedIn, and phone to engage candidates and clients.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted call notes and search
use_cases.description: Use AIRA Notetaker to summarize candidate calls and AIRA Search for natural-language candidate lookup.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Recruiterflow?
paragraphs: Recruiterflow is an AI-native applicant tracking system and CRM platform built for recruitment agencies and executive search firms. It combines candidate pipeline tracking with client relationship management, multichannel outreach sequences, and workflow automation.

The platform includes Recruiterflow AI, a suite of built-in intelligent agents, along with AIRA Search for natural-language search and AIRA Notetaker, which automatically summarizes key points from candidate calls. Recruiterflow reports SOC 2 and ISO 27001 certification along with GDPR compliance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Recruiterflow is for
paragraphs: Recruiterflow targets executive search firms, recruiting agencies, and staffing companies that need a combined ATS and CRM with automation and AI-assisted workflows. The company reports over 2,100 search firms globally as users.

enrichment.short_description: AI-first ATS and CRM for recruitment agencies, with AI sourcing, resume parsing, and native mobile apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Recruit CRM is an AI-first ATS and CRM for recruitment agencies, offering AI sourcing, resume parsing, and iOS and Android apps.

features.title: AI agents
features.description: Includes a resume formatting agent, an email reply agent that generates personalized replies, and a candidate submission agent that prepares structured submissions.

features.title: AI sourcing
features.description: Sources candidates on LinkedIn using natural-language queries.

features.title: AI candidate matching
features.description: Compares candidate profiles against job requirements using AI.

features.title: Chrome sourcing extension
features.description: Captures candidate profiles from LinkedIn, Xing, Gmail, and Outlook.

features.title: Resume parser
features.description: Converts PDF and Word resumes into structured candidate records.

features.title: Kanban pipeline view
features.description: Provides a visual, drag-and-drop view of candidate pipelines.

features.title: Job multiposting
features.description: Posts jobs to 5,000+ job boards from within the platform.

features.title: Workflow automation and integrations
features.description: Automates workflows and connects with 1,000+ third-party integrations.

pros: Rated 4.9/5 on Capterra, with top ratings on G2 and SoftwareAdvice
Offers native mobile apps for both Android and iOS
Unlimited free trial with no time limit (restricted premium features)
AI-first feature set including AI sourcing, matching, and resume formatting agents
Reports 1,000+ integrations and job multiposting to 5,000+ job boards
Used by agencies in 100+ countries, per the company
cons: Pricing is not published; the pricing page shows no dollar figures for Pro, Business, or Enterprise plans
Enterprise plan requires contacting the company for a custom quote
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Recruit CRM offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Recruit CRM offers an unlimited free trial with no time limit, though some premium features are restricted.

faqs.question: How much does Recruit CRM cost?
faqs.answer: Recruit CRM's pricing page lists Pro, Business, and Enterprise plans, but does not display specific dollar prices; monthly and annual billing are available, with up to 20% savings on annual billing.

faqs.question: Does Recruit CRM have mobile apps?
faqs.answer: Yes, Recruit CRM offers native mobile apps for Android and iOS in addition to its web platform.

faqs.question: Does Recruit CRM include AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes AI agents for resume formatting, email replies, and candidate submissions, plus AI-powered sourcing and candidate matching.

faqs.question: How many job boards can I post to with Recruit CRM?
faqs.answer: Recruit CRM supports job multiposting to 5,000+ job boards.

use_cases.title: Agency candidate sourcing and submissions
use_cases.description: Use AI sourcing and the Chrome extension to capture candidates from LinkedIn and other sources, then submit them to clients with AI-assisted formatting.

use_cases.title: Contract staffing pipeline management
use_cases.description: Track candidate pipelines with a Kanban view and manage job postings across thousands of job boards.

use_cases.title: Mobile recruiting on the go
use_cases.description: Manage candidates and pipelines from native iOS and Android apps.
use_cases.audience: Freelance recruiters and in-house recruiting teams

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Recruit CRM?
paragraphs: Recruit CRM describes itself as "the AI-first ATS + CRM that helps recruitment agencies place more candidates, faster." It combines AI agents, automation, and customizable workflows aimed at reducing administrative recruiting work.

The platform includes AI sourcing on LinkedIn with natural-language queries, AI candidate matching, a resume parser, a Chrome sourcing extension, and job multiposting to 5,000+ job boards, alongside native mobile apps for Android and iOS.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Recruit CRM is for
paragraphs: Recruit CRM is aimed at recruitment agencies, contract staffing firms, in-house recruiting teams, and freelance recruiters, with the company stating it is trusted by agency recruiters across 100+ countries.
